title: Best All-in-One ETFs in Canada 2026
description: Discover the best all-in-one ETFs in Canada for 2026. Compare top asset allocation funds like VGRO, VEQT, XGRO, and XEQT by returns, MER, and risk profile.

mainEntity.name: What is the best all-in-one ETF in Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: The best all-in-one ETF depends on your risk tolerance. For aggressive growth, VEQT (Vanguard) and XEQT (iShares) are top choices with 100% equity. For balanced portfolios, VBAL and XBAL offer a 60/40 mix of stocks and bonds.

mainEntity.name: VGRO vs XGRO: Which is better?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both are excellent 80/20 growth ETFs. VGRO has a slightly higher MER (0.24%) compared to XGRO (0.20%), but XGRO has historically maintained a slightly higher US allocation. The performance difference is typically minimal.

mainEntity.name: VEQT vs XEQT: Which all-equity ETF to choose?

acceptedAnswer.text: XEQT has a lower MER (0.20% vs VEQT's 0.24%) and a higher allocation to US equities (~45% vs ~40%). VEQT has a slightly higher allocation to Canadian equities (~30% vs ~25%). Choose based on your preference for home bias.

mainEntity.name: What is an asset allocation ETF?

acceptedAnswer.text: An asset allocation ETF (or all-in-one ETF) is a single fund that holds a globally diversified portfolio of stocks and/or bonds. It automatically rebalances to maintain a specific target asset mix (e.g., 80% stocks, 20% bonds), making investing incredibly simple.

mainEntity.name: Are Canadian all-in-one ETFs good for retirement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, they are excellent for retirement accounts like RRSPs and TFSAs. They offer instant global diversification, low fees compared to mutual funds, and automatic rebalancing, allowing for a hands-off, long-term investment strategy.
